name: Anti-Money Laundering Act (RA 9160, as amended)
citation: Republic Act No. 9160, as amended by RA 9194, RA 10167, RA 10365, RA 10927, RA 11521
issuingBody: Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) / Congress of the Philippines
summary: Requires covered institutions to file STRs and CTRs, implement KYC, and maintain transaction monitoring systems. Philippines exited FATF grey list in early 2025.
requirements: Suspicious Transaction Report (STR) filing to AMLC
Covered Transaction Report (CTR) filing for transactions ≥₱500,000
Know Your Customer (KYC) and Customer Due Diligence (CDD)
Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) for high-risk customers and PEPs
Transaction monitoring system capable of detecting ML/TF patterns
Record retention for minimum 5 years after account closure
PEP screening against domestic and international watchlists
penalties: Criminal penalties of 7-14 years imprisonment and fines of ₱3M-₱50M for non-compliance. AMLC can impose administrative sanctions and freeze orders.

sasCapabilityMapping.specificFeature: Automated regulatory report generation with AMLC format compliance
Scenario-based and ML-driven transaction monitoring with tunable thresholds
Risk-based customer scoring with automated EDD triggers
Real-time watchlist screening with fuzzy matching and alias resolution
Graph analytics for layering, structuring, and shell company detection

competitivePositioning: SAS AML is deployed at the largest banks globally and is recognized by Chartis as a category leader. Post-FATF grey list, Philippine institutions need to demonstrate sustained compliance — SAS provides the audit trail and reporting automation that regulators expect.
talkingPoints: Philippines exited FATF grey list in 2025 — sustained compliance is now the focus
AMLC is increasing examination frequency and depth post-grey-list
SAS AML handles both rule-based scenarios AND ML-driven anomaly detection
Network analytics is critical for detecting sophisticated layering schemes
SAS supports AMLC's specific reporting formats and filing requirements
Chartis RiskTech100 recognized SAS as #1 in AML solutions
